piece.title: Xanadu Voyage
piece.notes: Prog rock cinematic instrumental. E aeolian. Seven sections across ~8-9 minutes. Arc: atmosphere → motif birth → building complexity → climax → breakdown → recapitulation → dissolution. Layer Mellotron flutes on counterpoint track in Logic. ARP 2600 or Mini V lead over the melody track. Add a synth arpeggio manually in Logic over the harmony chords — the Xanadu-ish running 16th-note figure the engine cannot generate.

sections.name: prologue
sections.notes: Pure atmosphere. Pedal E drone underneath slowly shifting harmony. No motif — this is mist and space. Where the synth arpeggio enters manually in Logic. Very long, very spacious. Fade in from silence.

sections.name: awakening
sections.notes: Motif appears for the first time — sparse, tentative, like something stirring. The xanadu_call shape in its original form only. Bass stays minimal. No counterpoint yet. The ♭III (G) arrives for the first prog color moment.

sections.name: statement
sections.notes: Full motif statement. Counterpoint enters quietly on Mellotron flutes. Extended progression introduces iv (A minor) for darkness. Bass moves to root_fifth — gentle forward motion. Arc swells to midpoint climax. This is the first time the piece feels complete and alive.

sections.name: development
sections.notes: The engine works hardest here. Motif transforms — inversions, retrograde, augmentation, retrograde_inversion — across a long extended progression. Walking bass drives relentlessly. Counterpoint louder and more present. Two breath waves across 32 bars. Extended 9th and 11th chords. This is the prog center — complex, layered, earned.

sections.name: breakdown
sections.notes: The piece pulls back dramatically after the development peak. Sparse texture, pedal bass, long rests. Like the eye of the storm. No counterpoint. Sets up the recapitulation. The Mellotron strings thin out here — let them decay in Logic.

sections.name: recapitulation
sections.notes: Return of the motif at full force — but now the transforms have been heard, so the melody feels both familiar and evolved. Walking bass returns. Counterpoint re-enters. Arc swells to final climax before the coda. The dotted 8th delay on lead shines here.

sections.name: coda
sections.notes: Dissolution. Back to the opening harmonic simplicity — Em, D, C, Em. Very long chord durations (5 bars each). The motif appears once, maybe twice, then stops. Bass anchors on root only. No counterpoint. The piece exhales into silence. Let Valhalla Supermassive tail ring for 8+ seconds after the last note.
